project.name: Sylphx Consultant MCP
project.summary: Beta MCP server package for typed consultant review, research, answer challenge, and option comparison by autonomous agents.
goals: Provide the @sylphx/consultant-mcp package and sylphx-consultant-mcp stdio binary.
Own the four typed MCP consultant tool contracts and shared deliberation engine.
Keep provider fan-out, budget policy, privacy policy, redaction, schemas, docs, tests, and beta package verification coherent.
nonGoals: Own the future hosted Consultant Service, persistent ledger database, semantic cache, web UI, admin dashboard, or organization approval workflow.
Own model-vendor operations, enterprise doctrine, or customer-specific prompting policy.
Own downstream agent product behavior beyond the MCP package contract.

owns.name: consultant-mcp-tool-surface
owns.description: The stdio MCP server, CLI binary, typed tool schemas, server wiring, and package exports for consultant review, research, challenge, and option comparison.

owns.name: consultant-deliberation-engine
owns.description: The shared fan-out, judge synthesis, provider adapter, mock provider, policy, redaction, tests, docs, and beta package verification used by the MCP server.

doesNotOwn: Hosted Consultant Service APIs, persistent usage ledgers, async queues, semantic caches, web/admin dashboards, or organization approval workflows.
Model vendor account operations, customer-specific prompting policy, or downstream agent product behavior.
Enterprise engineering doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

publicSurfaces.name: Package README, usage guide, architecture, and beta ADR

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add hosted-service persistence, organization approval workflow, or customer-specific prompt policy to the beta MCP package boundary.
Do not make downstream agents depend on provider internals instead of the typed MCP tool contract.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: No repo-local GitHub CI workflow is present. Package release is through npm package scripts and future registry publication of the beta package.
delivery.productionProof: Run npm run verify, inspect npm pack --dry-run output, and read back the package registry/readme after published beta versions.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Before production publication, npm release intent must use Changesets or a repo-local ADR-59 equivalent for beta package versions.
packageRelease.versionPr: Version PRs must be bot or GitHub App-owned, with sylphx-release-bot as the default identity when installed.
packageRelease.publisher: Protected GitHub Actions release workflow should publish through OIDC/trusted publishing where supported, with any npm token kept as a bounded fallback.
packageRelease.publishProof: npm registry readback, package README readback, and provenance or attestation evidence for published beta package versions.

gaps.description: The repository has package verification scripts but no GitHub PR/merge_group CI workflow recorded at this baseline.
Published beta package versions are forward-only for consumers; add bad-release and registry recovery guidance before full adoption.
README intentionally defers hosted Consultant Service capabilities; a future service repo or ADR must own persistence, queues, cache, and admin UI before those features are built.
